feed.title: Shengdong (Shen) Zhao
feed.subtitle: Meet Shengdong (Shen) Zhao. I am a full professor at the School of Creative Media and Computer Science Department at the City University of Hong Kong (CityU).

entry.title: Visiting Three Star Mound(s) — Sanxingdui
entry.content:
During a recent outreach trip to Chengdu, I had the fortune of visiting the Sanxingdui Museum — home to the astonishing artifacts unearthed from the Three Star Mound site.

It was truly an eye-opening experience. The sheer number of beautiful and sophisticated artifacts on display speaks volumes about the impressive skills of people living in the Shu region of China around three thousand years ago. From towering bronze statues to intricate gold masks, the craftsmanship on display challenges many assumptions about ancient Chinese civilization.

If you ever find yourself in Chengdu, this museum is absolutely worth a visit. It offers a rare glimpse into a world that was, until recently, almost entirely unknown to modern scholarship.
entry.summary: During an outreach trip to Chengdu, a visit to the Sanxingdui Museum revealed astonishing artifacts from the ancient Shu civilization, dating back three thousand years.

entry.title: April 9 Running and Eating
entry.content:
We are happy to share that, thanks to the initiative of Rui and Yingyu, our Synteraction Lab weekly running and eating tradition is back in full swing!

On the evening of April 9th, lab members gathered for a refreshing run at the Joint Sports Center, followed by a wonderful dinner at Nanshan Village’s noodle shop. It was a perfect blend of exercise and camaraderie — exactly the kind of team bonding that keeps our lab spirit strong.

Everyone is welcome to join us for future sessions. Whether you’re looking for some outdoor exercise or simply want to hang out with the team over a good meal, we’d love to have you!
entry.summary: Thanks to Rui and Yingyu's initiative, Synteraction Lab's weekly running and eating tradition is back — with a refreshing evening run and noodle dinner.

entry.title: Lunch with Tony Tang in CityU’s Bistro Lodge

link.title: Lunch with Tony Tang in CityU’s Bistro Lodge

entry.content:
On April 8th, 2026, we had the pleasure of hosting Dr. Tony Tang from the Singapore Management University (SMU), where he directs the RICELab (Rethinking Interaction, Collaboration and Engagement) research group. Prof. Luo Yuhan and two of our students joined us for a wonderful lunch at CityU’s Bistro Lodge.

Tony’s research spans human-AI interaction, mixed reality interfaces, ubiquitous computing, and AI/robot-supported activity — making for rich common ground with our own work at the Synteraction Lab. Over lunch, we had an energizing discussion in which Tony shared his hands-on experiences with Claude Code and AI development. It was both thought-provoking and inspirational.

It’s always a delight to connect with fellow HCI researchers in the region, and we look forward to future collaborations and exchanges. Stay tuned!
entry.summary: A visit from Dr. Tony Tang of SMU led to energizing discussions on human-AI interaction over lunch at CityU's Bistro Lodge.

entry.title: Professor Shengdong Zhao Honored for Pioneering Contributions in HCI

link.title: Professor Shengdong Zhao Honored for Pioneering Contributions in HCI

entry.content: Professor Shengdong Zhao has been honored for his pioneering contributions in Human-Computer Interaction. This recognition celebrates his innovative research and dedication to advancing the field of HCI.
entry.summary: Congratulations to Professor Shengdong Zhao for being recognized for his pioneering contributions in Human-Computer Interaction.

entry.title: Award-Winning Research at ISS 2024
entry.content: Our team has been recognized for their award-winning research at the 2024 ACM Interactive Surfaces and Spaces (ISS) conference. This prestigious award highlights the innovative contributions our team has made to the field of interactive surfaces and spaces.
entry.summary: Congratulations to the team for their award-winning research presented at ISS 2024.

entry.title: Prof. Shengdong Zhao and Prof. Mark Billinghurst Dine in Adelaide

link.title: Prof. Shengdong Zhao and Prof. Mark Billinghurst Dine in Adelaide

entry.content: Prof. Shengdong Zhao and Prof. Mark Billinghurst enjoyed a delightful dinner together in Adelaide. The meeting provided an opportunity to discuss recent developments in human-computer interaction and augmented reality research.
entry.summary: Prof. Shengdong Zhao and Prof. Mark Billinghurst enjoyed a dinner together in Adelaide.

entry.title: UIST 2024: GPTVoiceTasker: Advancing Multi-step Mobile Task Efficiency Through Dynamic Interface Exploration and Learning

link.title: UIST 2024: GPTVoiceTasker: Advancing Multi-step Mobile Task Efficiency Through Dynamic Interface Exploration and Learning

entry.content:
!Congratulations to the authors of “GPTVoiceTasker: Advancing Multi-step Mobile Task Efficiency Through Dynamic Interface Exploration and Learning”. This paper has been accepted to UIST’24.

Authors

Minh Duc Vu, Han Wang, Zhuang Li, Jieshan Chen, Shengdong Zhao, Zhenchang Xing, Chunyang Chen

Abstract

Virtual assistants have the potential to play an important role in helping users achieves different tasks. However, these systems face challenges in their real-world usability, characterized by inefficiency and struggles in grasping user intentions. Leveraging recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs), we introduce GptVoiceTasker, a virtual assistant poised to enhance user experiences and task efficiency on mobile devices. GptVoiceTasker excels at intelligently deciphering user commands and executing relevant device interactions to streamline task completion. The system continually learns from historical user commands to automate subsequent usages, further enhancing execution efficiency. Our experiments affirm GptVoiceTasker’s exceptional command interpretation abilities and the precision of its task automation module. In our user study, GptVoiceTasker boosted task efficiency in real-world scenarios by 34.85%, accompanied by positive participant feedback. We made GptVoiceTasker open-source, inviting further research into LLMs utilization for diverse tasks through prompt engineering and leveraging user usage data to improve efficiency.
entry.summary: Congratulations to the authors of GPTVoiceTasker: Advancing Multi-step Mobile Task Efficiency Through Dynamic Interface Exploration and Learning.

entry.title: The Conclusion of the 2024 Chiang Mai Research Camp

link.title: The Conclusion of the 2024 Chiang Mai Research Camp

entry.content: Over the course of several weeks, participants from diverse academic backgrounds came together to explore new frontiers in environmental science, technology, and cultural studies. The camp fostered a vibrant exchange of ideas, culminating in groundbreaking projects that promise to have a lasting impact. As attendees bid farewell, they left with not only valuable knowledge and experience but also a renewed commitment to their respective fields, inspired by the unique blend of tradition and modernity that Chiang Mai offers.
entry.summary: The end of the 2024 Chiang Mai Research Camp was marked by a celebration of innovation and collaboration.

entry.title: Synteraction Lab’s Thriving Weekly Running Event

link.title: Synteraction Lab’s Thriving Weekly Running Event

entry.content:
Synteraction Lab’s weekly running event saw another successful turnout this Thursday.

Lab members gathered at the local track, showcasing their enthusiasm and commitment to fitness. The event continues to foster camaraderie and promote healthy lifestyles among team members.
entry.summary: Synteraction Lab's weekly running event saw another successful turnout this Thursday

entry.title: UbiComp/ISWC 2024 Workshop on Heads-Up Computing
entry.content:
Join us at Ubicomp 2024 for an immersive workshop on Heads-Up Computing, where we’ll navigate the future of wearable and pervasive technology — all innovators and thought leaders are welcome!

The UbiComp/ISWC 2024 workshop will focus on “Heads-Up Computing,” exploring the future of wearable intelligent assistants like Apple’s Vision Pro, Rayban Meta Glasses, and XReal’s Air smartglasses. This event will address design challenges, user interaction, privacy, and ethics in integrating these devices into daily life.

The workshop invites academics and industry professionals to submit papers by July 5, 2024, with the event scheduled for October 5-6 in Melbourne, Australia.

For more details, visit the workshop website.
entry.summary: Opportunities and Challenges of the Next Interaction Paradigm with Wearable Intelligent Assistants
